Assembly: The assembly ilNemBell1.hap1.1 is based on 43x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). The single copy Z chromosome in the diploid map with no evidence of a W in the assembly indicates that this sample is of the ZO karyotype. Order and orientation of scaffolds on chromosome 8 from ~17.75-18.01Mb, chromosome 22 from ~12.51-12.64Mb is unsure.

Assembly: The assembly aEpiCal4.hap2.1 is based on 44x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The following regions are highly repetitive and the order and orientation are uncertain: Chromosome 1 HAP1 from 234.77 to 262.91 Mbp and Chromosome 2 HAP1 from 334.88 to 341.73 Mbp, Chromosome 1 HAP2 from 233.92 to 254.08 Mbp. This genome was assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased, resulting in hap1 and hap2 curated genomes.

Description: Melinaea menophilus n. ssp. AW-2005 genome assembly, chromosome: 2
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Papilionoidea; Nymphalidae; Danainae; Ithomiini; Melinaeina; Melinaea
Assembly: The assembly ilMelMeno1.hap2.4 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The 19 autosomes in Hap1 are named after homology to the previous assembly version. There is a fusion between the W and the previously named autosome 5, the resulting fused chromosome is named W. The non-fused homologous autosome in named Z2. The canonical Z named Z. The 21 autosomes in Hap2 are named based on homology to Hap1. There are 2 heterozygous fission/fusions, the non-fused chromosomes are placed in Hap2 named 1A, 1B, 4A and 4B. Hap1 autosome 2 35.7-37.9 Mb shows high sequence identity to a part of autosome 17 (both haplotypes), likely duplication. This duplication is not present in the homologous Hap2 autosome 2A.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Tracheophyta; Spermatophyta; Magnoliopsida; eudicotyledons; Gunneridae; Pentapetalae; asterids; lamiids; Gentianales; Rubiaceae; Rubioideae; Rubieae; Galium
Assembly: This project provides the tetraploid genome assembly daGalBore2.1 of the northern bedstraw, Galium boreale. The sample was provided by Thomas Marcussen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). daGalBore2.1 is based on 41x PacBio HiFi data and 53x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly daGalBore2.1 of the northern bedstraw, Galium boreale. The sample was provided by Thomas Marcussen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). daGalBore2.1 is based on 41x PacBio HiFi data and 53x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Diogenes pugilator. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Diogenes pugilator (qmDioPugi1) is based on 34X ONT data and 584X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 52 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 9.978 Mb (with the largest being 0.383 Mb). Additionally, 2216 regions totaling 340.821 Mb (with the largest being 0.816 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 9 haplotypic regions, totaling 1.8Mb, (with the largest being 0.3Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilCalHemi2.hap1.1 is based on 79x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. Chromosome 2 10.0 - 12.1 Mbp region is duplicated on chromosome 26.

Assembly: The assembly ilMelMars1.hap2.3 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Hap1 have 13 autosomes named after size, hap2 have 14 autosomes that are named based on homology with hap1. Super_1 in hap1 is split in two parts in hap2 (Super_1A, Super_1B). Assignment of sex-chromosomes due to they being hemizygous and synteny of Z with the canonical Z.

Assembly: The assembly ilMelMoth8.hap2.1 is based on 41x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The autosomes in Hap1 are named by size, and the autosomes in Hap2 are named based on homology to Hap1. Chromosome 2 in Hap1 consists of two chromosomes in Hap2 (Super_2A, Super_2B).The sexchromosomes are named Z1, Z2, W1 and W2. Z1 in named based on synteny to the canonical Z, W1 is involved with an autosome fusion and the homologous autosome named Z2. The W2 is a non-fused part homologus to part of Z2.The order and orientation of the highly repetitive contigs in W1 2-4 Mb and 16.6-19.1 Mb, Hap1 Super_7 27-28.5 Mb and Hap1 Super_8 0-2.1Mb are uncertain.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Echinodermata; Eleutherozoa; Echinozoa; Holothuroidea; Aspidochirotacea; Aspidochirotida; Holothuriidae; Actinopyga
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Echinodermata; Eleutherozoa; Echinozoa; Holothuroidea; Aspidochirotacea; Aspidochirotida; Holothuriidae; Actinopyga
Assembly: The assembly of Actinopyga flammea (ehActFlam1) is based on 46X PacBio data and 162X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 12 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.828 Mb (with the largest being 0.912 Mb). Additionally, 273 regions totaling 75.36 Mb (with the largest being 3.052 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 12 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 12,96Mb (with the largest being 2.70Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

In total, 12 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 1.828 Mb (with the largest being 0.912 Mb). Additionally, 273 regions totaling 75.36 Mb (with the largest being 3.052 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 12 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 12,96Mb (with the largest being 2.70Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly of Diogenes curvimanus (qmDioCurv1.1) is based on 37X ONT data and 231X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 50 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 9.418 Mb (with the largest being 1.769 Mb). Additionally, 3293 regions totaling 174.142 Mb (with the largest being 0.385 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed.The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. A linear sequence of 35Kb was obtained. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 300 haplotypic regions and 5 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 67.859 Mb and 0.111 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 0.955 Mb and 0.032 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly daGalBore1.hap1.1 is based on 60x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The sample is tetraploid (4n = 44). Hap1 is a chromosome-level curated assembly. The other 3 haplotypes are in HAP2 file and are scaffold-level curated. Some haplotypic inversions in HAP1 were detected on: Chromosome 2 from 13.02 to 15.86, 19.37 to 24.58, 19.37 to 26.24, 31.51 to 34.80 and 35.26 to 38.44 Mbp, Chromosome 9 from 21.86 to 32.90 Mbp and Chromosome 10 from 21.98 to 23.44 Mbp.
